channel.title: Home on hexagonally.mired
channel.description: Recent content in Home on hexagonally.mired

item.title: March 13, 2026
item.description:
It’s been six years now, since life froze for the greatest propagandist empire ever. Following the first case in a senior center in Kent, WA, the machine froze fast, and we saw our carbon output slow down for just a little.

Today, six years, later, I Wake up to the rhythm of the city and I try to remember, even my best friends have some trouble with each other, sometimes things fall apart.

item.title: March 12, 2026
item.description:
Music Recs!!!

Morgan Nagler

if you’ve got nothing to listen to on your way back, and the time is past 9p, I think you’ll be able to listen to Morgan Nagler’s ‘I’ve Got Nothing To Lose, and I’m Losing It’ the full album releases at midnight Eastern Daylight, and (at least on spotify) it drops at 2100.

She co-wrote Phoebe Bridgers’ Kyoto, and the songs from the album she’s already dropped are super fun

item.title: February 21, 2026:
item.description:
My view from the end of a warped tunnel.

I sit

I sit at the end of a warped tunnel, in fact!

I’ve primed myself for the dim that comes

when someone sends me sights

which have touched their soul

In the dim darkness,

I become a furnace,

Cold and still nervous

Because without the broken camera of another soul

I’ve become poised to be lost in my own fishbowl

item.title: Inital Hello!
item.description:
Hi, Grace!

Um yeah that’s it

I have so much to say, but it’s all locked in my head in a different language.

Instead, you get an image

item.title: Thing
item.description: Here’s another cool picture I took

item.title: February 20, 2026
item.description:
Hey, Grace; Could you tell that the smile in that picture I sent you that one time is actually RED?? I had no idea until I went there again a few weeks later :o

Here’s the picture for reference:

Here’s another cool picture I took:

item.title: Hi
item.description: This is what everyone wants to see, I hope..

item.title: Weekend Check-In, 2026/2/2
item.description: I’m not sure what Advanced Placement® Human Geography is supposed to teach you. To the contrary, what I am sure of is that it’s not supposed to teach you to discard the notion of original thought. However, after a semester in this course, a timely breakup, and the just as timely introduction of a book to my life, I’ve discovered just this. Original thought is the ultimate fiction of man. The goal of a human is to make it impossible for others to forget them, while the goal of humanity is to make it possible for others to forget us. This is, as a highly educated intellectual would say, ‘hella contradictory ideology.’ Thus, I’d like to put forward the idea that each individual is, in the large scheme of things, meant to be forgotten. Their ideas are intended to be turned into the mass amalgam that is human consciousness. Globalization is the inevitable path of the world, and now that we are here, it seems the minds of the many have not caught up. We must discard the idea that our ideas are meant to be ours, forever, and give all credit to the mass consciousness (if it must be god then ugh, fine).

item.title: The Premier Post
item.description:
Hiya! You’ve found the first post in my /posts page.

I’m not actually sure what to do with this folder, but I don’t want other projects to take up space here. Have a nice day!!

PLEASE JUST SHOW UP LOL

item.title: The Anthropocene Reviewed
item.description:
My notes on John Green’s The Anthropocene Reviewed

On humans and lemmings:

“Trial thought experiment (The word trial is used here because I have 100% certainty that nobody on Earth has ever seen this account.): I’m going to highlight this phrase(call me french, i darest thou) that so evidently corroborates our opinion of humanity. Nobody that sees this, or the quote causing it, will get triggered (to those living in the world of parentheses and semantics i say, shut up.), because we are so convinced that humanity is horrific and irredeemable. To you, I say, shut up (as well). Humanity is redeemable. Go redeem it. Heck, that could be our new slogan. Redeem us, or go extinct

item.title: Weekend Check-In, 2026/2/2
item.description: I’m not sure what Advanced Placement® Human Geography is supposed to teach you. To the contrary, what I am sure of is that it’s not supposed to teach you to discard the notion of original thought. However, after a semester in this course, a timely breakup, and the just as timely introduction of a book to my life, I’ve discovered just this. Original thought is the ultimate fiction of man. The goal of a human is to make it impossible for others to forget them, while the goal of humanity is to make it possible for others to forget us. This is, as a highly educated intellectual would say, ‘hella contradictory ideology.’ Thus, I’d like to put forward the idea that each individual is, in the large scheme of things, meant to be forgotten. Their ideas are intended to be turned into the mass amalgam that is human consciousness. Globalization is the inevitable path of the world, and now that we are here, it seems the minds of the many have not caught up. We must discard the idea that our ideas are meant to be ours, forever, and give all credit to the mass consciousness (if it must be god then ugh, fine).
